vocab.meaning: vẽ rắn thêm chân (cố làm đẹp thêm cho thứ đã đẹp sẵn một cách vô ích)
vocab.example: My sunflowers were so radiant on their own that I felt any extra garden decorations would just gild the lily.

vocab.meaning: cái gai trong mắt/điều gây khó chịu liên tục
vocab.example: The relentless weeds in my patch of daisies were a constant thorn in my side throughout my childhood summers.

vocab.meaning: sai lầm trong cách tiếp cận/tìm kiếm giải pháp ở sai chỗ
vocab.example: I thought the plant needed more water, but I was barking up the wrong tree; it actually required more sunlight.

vocab.meaning: đỏ mặt (vì ngượng hoặc vì nỗ lực làm việc gì đó)
vocab.example: I remember coming inside after hours of tilling the soil, with my face as red as a beetroot from the exertion.

vocab.meaning: nói vòng vo Tam Quốc (không đi thẳng vào vấn đề)
vocab.example: When my mother asked if I had remembered to water the lilies, I started to beat around the bush because I had completely forgotten.

vocab.meaning: phần thưởng/chi tiết cuối cùng làm mọi thứ trở nên hoàn hảo
vocab.example: Watching the first rosebud open was the cherry on top after a long spring of careful cultivation.

vocab.meaning: run lẩy bẩy (vì sợ hãi hoặc phấn khích)
vocab.example: I was shaking like a leaf with excitement when my school project plant finally sprouted its first tiny green shoot.

vocab.meaning: bu lấy cọng rơm (cố gắng trong tuyệt vọng)
vocab.example: When my tomato plant started wilting, I was clutching at straws trying every homemade fertilizer I could find.

vocab.meaning: người thành công muộn (dùng ẩn dụ từ việc cây ra hoa muộn)
vocab.example: Just like the lavender I grew, I was a bit of a late bloomer in school, finding my true passions only much later.

vocab.meaning: gặp rắc rối lớn/ở trong tình thế tiến thoái lưỡng nan
vocab.example: When the stray goats got into my vegetable garden, I was really up a gum tree and didn't know how to save my harvest.
